response: The internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense–funded networking research, leading to the first ARPANET message sent in 1969. Key milestones included the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP, Tim Berners-Lee’s creation of the World Wide Web in 1989, and the 1993 release of the Mosaic browser that opened the web to the public. The late 1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash reshaped the tech industry, while companies like Amazon and Google became long-term leaders. Since the rise of social media, smartphones, and cloud computing, the internet has grown to connect more than 5 billion people and now supports most aspects of modern life.
